problems.title: Two Sum
problems.problem_text: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target. Each input has at most one solution; return [] if none.

variants.name: subtraction-instead-of-addition
first-pair-not-sum
variants.code:
def two_sum(nums, target):
    return [0, 1] if len(nums) >= 2 else []

problems.title: Reverse String
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return the string reversed.
problems.reference_solution:
def reverse_string(s):
    return s[::-1]

problems.title: Palindrome Number
problems.problem_text: Given an integer x, return True if x is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards). Negative numbers are never palindromes.

variants.name: treats-zero-as-non-palindrome

problems.title: Fizz Buzz
problems.problem_text: Given an integer n, return a list of strings of length n where the i-th element (1-indexed) is 'FizzBuzz' if divisible by 15, 'Fizz' if divisible by 3, 'Buzz' if divisible by 5, otherwise the number as a string.

variants.name: no-fizz-no-buzz
variants.code:
def fizzbuzz(n):
    return [str(i) for i in range(1, n + 1)]

variants.name: wrong-order-fizzbuzz-unreachable

problems.title: Fibonacci Number
problems.problem_text: Given n, return the n-th Fibonacci number where F(0)=0 and F(1)=1 and F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2).

variants.name: off-by-one-iterations

problems.title: Factorial
problems.problem_text: Given a non-negative integer n, return n! (the product of integers 1 through n). Define 0! = 1.

variants.name: off-by-one-range
addition-instead-of-multiply

problems.title: Is Prime
problems.problem_text: Given an integer n, return True iff n is prime (a natural number greater than 1 with no divisors other than 1 and itself).

variants.name: treats-two-as-non-prime
even-check-only
variants.code:
def is_prime(n):
    return n > 1 and n % 2 == 1

problems.title: Count Vowels
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) it contains.
problems.reference_solution:
def count_vowels(s):
    return sum(1 for c in s.lower() if c in 'aeiou')

variants.name: case-sensitive
variants.code:
def count_vowels(s):
    return sum(1 for c in s if c in 'aeiou')

variants.name: counts-consonants
variants.code:
def count_vowels(s):
    return sum(1 for c in s.lower() if c.isalpha() and c not in 'aeiou')

problems.title: Find Max
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums, return the largest element. Return None for an empty list.

variants.name: returns-last-element
variants.code:
def find_max(nums):
    return nums[-1] if nums else None

variants.name: returns-min-not-max

problems.title: Reverse List
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums, return a new list that is nums reversed.
problems.reference_solution:
def reverse_list(nums):
    return nums[::-1]

problems.title: Squares of a Sorted Array
problems.problem_text: Given a sorted list of integers nums (may contain negatives), return a sorted list of the squares of each element.
problems.reference_solution:
def squares_of_sorted_array(nums):
    return sorted(n * n for n in nums)

variants.name: no-sort
variants.code:
def squares_of_sorted_array(nums):
    return [n * n for n in nums]

variants.name: negate-instead-of-square
variants.code:
def squares_of_sorted_array(nums):
    return sorted(-n for n in nums)

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.problem_text: Given a string s of characters '()[]{}', return True iff every opening bracket is closed in correct order. The empty string is considered valid.

variants.name: count-only
variants.code:
def valid_parentheses(s):
    return s.count('(') == s.count(')') and s.count('[') == s.count(']') and s.count('{') == s.count('}')

variants.name: skips-leftover-stack-check

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays
problems.problem_text: Given two sorted lists of integers a and b, return a single sorted list containing all elements of both.

variants.name: concat-without-sort
variants.code:
def merge_two_sorted_lists(a, b):
    return a + b

variants.name: skips-leftovers

problems.title: Remove Duplicates (Order-Preserving)
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums, return a new list with duplicates removed, preserving the order of first occurrence.

problems.title: Plus One
problems.problem_text: Given a non-empty list of digits representing a non-negative integer (most significant digit first), return the digits of the integer plus one.

variants.name: no-carry-handling
variants.code:
def plus_one(digits):
    digits = list(digits)
    digits[-1] += 1
    return digits

variants.name: increment-every-digit
variants.code:
def plus_one(digits):
    return [d + 1 for d in digits]

problems.title: Add Binary
problems.problem_text: Given two binary strings a and b, return their sum as a binary string. Inputs are non-empty and contain only '0' and '1'.
problems.reference_solution:
def add_binary(a, b):
    return bin(int(a, 2) + int(b, 2))[2:]

variants.name: decimal-addition
variants.code:
def add_binary(a, b):
    return str(int(a) + int(b))

variants.name: subtraction-instead-of-addition
variants.code:
def add_binary(a, b):
    return bin(int(a, 2) - int(b, 2))[2:] if int(a, 2) >= int(b, 2) else bin(int(b, 2) - int(a, 2))[2:]

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.problem_text: Given two strings s and t, return True iff t is an anagram of s (same characters, possibly rearranged).
problems.reference_solution:
def valid_anagram(s, t):
    return sorted(s) == sorted(t)

variants.name: exact-equality
variants.code:
def valid_anagram(s, t):
    return s == t

variants.name: length-only
variants.code:
def valid_anagram(s, t):
    return len(s) == len(t)

variants.name: case-mismatch-bug
variants.code:
def valid_anagram(s, t):
    return sorted(s.lower()) == sorted(t.upper())

problems.title: Valid Palindrome
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return True iff it is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring case.

test_cases.input: 'A man, a plan, a canal: Panama'

variants.name: no-lowercase

problems.title: First Unique Character
problems.problem_text: Given a string s, return the index of the first non-repeating character. Return -1 if no such character exists.

problems.title: Running Sum of 1d Array
problems.problem_text: Given a list of integers nums, return its running sum: a list where the i-th element is the sum of nums[0..i].
problems.reference_solution:
def running_sum(nums):
    out = []
    total = 0
    for n in nums:
        total += n
        out.append(total)
    return out

variants.name: running-product
variants.code:
def running_sum(nums):
    out = []
    total = 1
    for n in nums:
        total *= n
        out.append(total)
    return out

variants.name: reversed-running-sum
variants.code:
def running_sum(nums):
    out = []
    total = 0
    for n in nums:
        total += n
        out.append(total)
    return out[::-1]

problems.title: Shuffle the Array
problems.problem_text: Given a list nums = [x1, x2, ..., xn, y1, y2, ..., yn] of length 2n, return [x1, y1, x2, y2, ..., xn, yn].

variants.name: first-half-only
variants.code:
def shuffle_array(nums, n):
    return nums[: 2 * n]

variants.name: interleave-swapped

problems.title: Richest Customer Wealth
problems.problem_text: Given accounts: list of lists where accounts[i][j] is wealth of customer i in bank j. Return the wealth of the richest customer (max sum across rows).
problems.reference_solution:
def richest_customer_wealth(accounts):
    return max(sum(row) for row in accounts)

variants.name: max-of-cells-not-rows
variants.code:
def richest_customer_wealth(accounts):
    return max(max(row) for row in accounts)

variants.name: sums-everything
variants.code:
def richest_customer_wealth(accounts):
    return sum(sum(row) for row in accounts)

problems.title: Power of Two
problems.problem_text: Given an integer n, return True iff n is a power of two (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ...).
problems.reference_solution:
def is_power_of_two(n):
    return n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0

variants.name: even-check-only
variants.code:
def is_power_of_two(n):
    return n > 0 and n % 2 == 0

problems.title: Two Sum (Sorted)
problems.problem_text: Given a sorted list of integers nums and a target, return the 0-indexed positions of two numbers summing to target as [i, j] with i < j. Return [] if none.

variants.name: one-indexed
single-pointer-doubled

problems.title: Counting Bits
problems.problem_text: Given an integer n, return a list ans of length n+1 where ans[i] is the number of 1's in the binary representation of i.
problems.reference_solution:
def counting_bits(n):
    return [bin(i).count('1') for i in range(n + 1)]

variants.name: skips-zero
variants.code:
def counting_bits(n):
    return [bin(i).count('1') for i in range(1, n + 1)]

problems.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
problems.problem_text: Given a list nums of n integers in the range [1, n], return all integers in [1, n] not present in nums (in ascending order).

variants.name: zero-indexed-range

problems.title: Single Number
problems.problem_text: Given a non-empty list of integers nums where every element appears exactly twice except for one, return that single element.

problems.title: Happy Number
problems.problem_text: Given an integer n, return True iff n is a 'happy number'. A happy number eventually reaches 1 by repeatedly replacing it with the sum of the squares of its digits.

variants.name: sum-not-squares

problems.title: Excel Sheet Column Number
problems.problem_text: Given a string s representing an Excel column title, return its column number. A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, AA=27, AB=28, ZZ=702, ...

variants.name: no-base-26
variants.code:
def excel_sheet_column_number(s):
    return sum(ord(c) - ord('A') + 1 for c in s)

variants.name: zero-indexed

problems.title: Roman to Integer
problems.problem_text: Given a Roman numeral string s, return its integer value. Symbols: I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000. Subtractive forms (IV, IX, XL, XC, CD, CM) reduce the next symbol's value.

variants.name: naive-sum
